versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 29 Then Jacob instructed them, “I am about to be gathered to my people. Bury me with my fathers in the cave in the field of Ephron the Hittite. 30 The cave is in the field of Machpelah near Mamre, in the land of Canaan. This is the field Abraham purchased from Ephron the Hittite as a burial site. 31 There Abraham and his wife Sarah are buried, there Isaac and his wife Rebekah are buried, and there I buried Leah. 32 The field and the cave that is in it were purchased from the Hittites.”
33 When Jacob had finished instructing his sons, he pulled his feet into the bed and breathed his last, and he was gathered to his people.

context_lines: In **Genesis 49**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Genesis.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **The Death of Jacob** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Genesis 49** remains the first control on meaning.
